raw_consumer_statement:
My Citi replacement credit card was stolen from my mail/ in the postal system and activated and used at various retail stores fraudulently from XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/XXXX, with total fraud charges adding up to $ XXXX {$2500.00}. I learned about this theft and alerted Citi on XX/XX/XXXX. A supervisor at Citi told me to to send in a police report and my ID through email. I then went to the XXXX police precinct in XXXX, NY to try to file a police report, where police told me they required a written/ notarized affidavit from Citibank in order to open a police report to this matter. I then called Citi again to try to obtain this written affidavit, spoke to XXXX different agents/ supervisors and spent many hours on the phone. I was transferred from agent to agent, from department to department, however, could not get anyone who could get me this affidavit or resolve this issue in an alternative way. 

I then waited for Citibank to " investigate '' this case and give me further instructions of how to proceed. Since Citibank refusd to provide this document I could not obtain a police report. XXXX XXXX  passed without any notification from Citi. Then I received a letter dated XX/XX/XXXX from Citibank stating that the investigation was closed and I was responsible for all fraud charges, because I did not provide the police report. I then went to precinct XXXX for a XXXX time and spoke with a different officer, who was equally rigid about the written affidavit requirement and would not accept any other document including bank statement etc. I then called Citi again at the police precinct, spent hours on the phone being transferred from person to person, not getting through to anyone willing or able to provide this document, or tell me what alternative document could be used in place of the police report. The police even spoke to the reps to clarify what is needed, however, Citi employees remain thoroughly confused and/or uncooperative. 

Citibank credit card department has a unacceptably poor fraud detection / protection system, dysfuntional customer service, and is uninterested in fairly or properly investigating fraudulent activity. They disregard consumer protection laws and will hold customers responsible at the first opportunity. 

I appealed this case and asked for it to be reopened with proper communication and alternative resolution.
